skill.name: specdd-task
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to find, add, interpret, organize, or update SpecDD `Tasks` entries.
skill.summary: Manages SpecDD task entries in .sdd files with proper spec chain resolution and task status tracking.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The skill is a pure markdown documentation file with no executable code. Static analyzer flagged 18 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns and 2 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings - all are false positives. The backticks are markdown inline code formatting for identifiers like `Tasks`, `.sdd`, `specdd-adopt`, and status markers like `[x]`, `[!]`, `[?]`. The crypto reference relates to SHA256 content hashes in the report schema, not weak algorithms in the skill. No scripts, network access, or external commands are present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified 18 instances of backticks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are actually markdown inline code formatting for file names, task status markers, and skill references. The file contains no executable code.

content.user_title: Manage SpecDD Task Entries with Confidence
content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need consistent task management across .sdd files. This skill provides clear rules for finding, adding, and updating task entries while respecting spec chain authority.
actual_capabilities: Find existing task entries in SpecDD .sdd files
Add new local, actionable tasks consistent with inherited constraints
Update task status using standard markers [x], [!], [?], [-]
Resolve the effective spec chain for a given target
Identify the nearest local spec granting write authority before editing
Report task IDs, status changes, and blocked or uncertain items
limitations: Only works with projects that have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Cannot be used to override Must, Must not, Forbids, or write authority rules
Does not handle bootstrap creation - use specdd-adopt for new SpecDD projects
Task text must remain concise and implementation-oriented

use_cases.title: Adding implementation tasks to a SpecDD spec
use_cases.description: When working on a SpecDD project feature, use this skill to add new tasks to the appropriate .sdd file while respecting the spec chain and inherited constraints.

use_cases.title: Tracking and reporting task status
use_cases.description: Use this skill to mark tasks as complete, blocked, or skipped, and to generate status reports listing task IDs and changes for project reviews.

use_cases.title: Resolving spec chain authority before edits
use_cases.description: When planning or editing work in a SpecDD project, use this skill to properly resolve the spec chain and confirm write authority before making changes.
use_cases.target_user: AI agents working in SpecDD codebases

prompt_templates.title: Find tasks in a specific spec file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-task skill to find all task entries in the .specdd/features/auth.sdd file and list their current statuses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing existing work in a SpecDD project

prompt_templates.title: Add a new implementation task
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the specdd-task skill, add a new task to the .specdd/features/api.sdd file for implementing rate limiting middleware. Ensure it is consistent with inherited constraints.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning new work in a SpecDD spec

prompt_templates.title: Mark tasks complete after verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the specdd-task skill to mark task #3 in .specdd/features/auth.sdd as complete [x] now that the implementation and tests are verified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Updating task status after finishing work

prompt_templates.title: Generate a task status report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-task skill to generate a report of all task status changes in the .specdd/ directory, including any blocked or uncertain items.

output: Task #3: [?] Choose session storage backend (Redis vs PostgreSQL)
Task #4: [!] Blocked - waiting for security review approval

output_examples.input: Add a new task for implementing password reset flow
output_examples.output: Added task to .specdd/features/auth.sdd: Task #5: [-] Skipped - deferred to v2.0 release scope. The task respects inherited constraints from bootstrap.md and does not override write authority.

best_practices: Always resolve the spec chain before editing any task entries
Identify the nearest local spec that grants write authority before any edit
Mark tasks complete only after the work and relevant checks are fully done
Use [!] for blocked work and [!] for unresolved design decisions to signal status clearly
anti_patterns: Do not use tasks to override Must, Must not, Forbids, or write authority rules
Do not complete unrelated tasks while handling a requested task
Do not infer write authority from similar filenames or module names - always check the governing spec

faq.question: What projects does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill only works with projects that have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file. For non-SpecDD projects, use the specdd-adopt skill instead.

faq.question: How do I mark a task as blocked?
faq.answer: Use the [!] status marker for blocked work. Use [?] for unresolved design decisions and [-] only for intentionally skipped tasks with a clear reason.

faq.question: Can this skill create or modify bootstrap files?
faq.answer: No. This skill handles task entries only. Use the specdd-adopt skill to add or adopt SpecDD in new or existing projects.

faq.question: What is the spec chain resolution order?
faq.answer: Resolve bootstrap.md first, then bootstrap.project.md, then bootstrap.local.md. Then walk ancestor specs from the content root to the target, including same-directory basename specs when applicable.

faq.question: Can I infer write authority from file names?
faq.answer: No. Do not infer authority from similar filenames, nearby files, symbols, or module names. Always check the active bootstrap or a governing spec for explicit authority mapping.

faq.question: What should be included in a task report?
faq.answer: List task IDs or exact task text, status changes, verification results, and any task that remains blocked or uncertain.
